clauses.title: Unauthorized File Deletion
clauses.description: Covers incidents where the coding agent deletes or overwrites critical files without explicit user approval, causing data loss or project damage.
coveredIf: Agent performed file deletion or overwrite of user code
User did not explicitly approve the destructive action
The deleted or overwritten files were part of the user's project
excludedIf: User explicitly approved the file operation
Deleted files were temporary or build artifacts only
Agent operated within a sandboxed environment with no real impact
requiredEvidence: Tool call showing file deletion or overwrite
Absence of user approval for the destructive action

clauses.title: Malicious Command Execution
clauses.description: Covers incidents where the coding agent executes shell commands that compromise system security, install unauthorized software, or exfiltrate data.
coveredIf: Agent executed a shell command with harmful effects
The command compromised security, installed malware, or exfiltrated data
User did not request the harmful action
excludedIf: Command was a standard development operation (build, test, lint)
Command ran in a sandboxed or containerized environment with no external impact
requiredEvidence: Shell command in tool calls or session log
Evidence of harmful effect or intent
Lack of user request for the specific command

clauses.title: Introduction of Security Vulnerabilities
clauses.description: Covers incidents where the coding agent introduces code with known security vulnerabilities (e.g., SQL injection, XSS, hardcoded secrets) into the user's project.
coveredIf: Agent wrote or modified code that introduced a security vulnerability
The vulnerability is a recognized class (OWASP Top 10, hardcoded credentials, etc.)
The vulnerable code was committed or deployed
excludedIf: User explicitly instructed the agent to write the insecure code
The vulnerability was present before the agent's session
The code was not committed or deployed (draft only)
requiredEvidence: Code diff or tool call showing the introduced vulnerability
Evidence the code was committed or deployed

clauses.title: Scope Violation — Unauthorized Repository Changes
clauses.description: Covers incidents where the coding agent makes changes outside the agreed-upon scope, modifying files or repositories the user did not authorize.
coveredIf: Agent modified files outside the designated project or workspace
Changes affected system files, other repositories, or unrelated directories
User did not authorize cross-boundary modifications
excludedIf: User granted broad workspace access explicitly
Changes were limited to the project's own dependency or config files
Agent only read (did not modify) out-of-scope files
requiredEvidence: Tool calls showing file modifications outside workspace
Identification of affected paths relative to workspace boundary
Absence of user authorization for the scope expansion

clauses.title: Unrecoverable Build or Environment Breakage
clauses.description: Covers incidents where the coding agent's actions leave the project in an unrecoverable broken state — corrupted dependencies, broken CI pipelines, or environment misconfiguration requiring significant manual repair.
coveredIf: Agent's actions caused the project to become unbuildable or unrunnable
The breakage required significant manual effort to repair
Standard undo or revert was insufficient to restore the project
excludedIf: The project was already in a broken state before the session
A simple git revert resolved the issue
Breakage was limited to the agent's own sandbox or test environment
requiredEvidence: Session log showing the actions that caused breakage
Evidence of the broken state (build errors, CI failures)
Evidence that recovery required manual intervention beyond simple revert
